item.title: Bhikkhus, possessing three qualities, a friend should be associated with. What three? Here, bhikkhus, a bhikkhu gives what is hard to give, does what is…
item.description:
Bhikkhus, possessing three qualities, a friend should be associated with. What three? Here, bhikkhus, a bhikkhu gives what is hard to give, does what is hard to do, endures what is hard to endure. Bhikkhus, possessing these three qualities, a friend should be associated with.

From: Aṅguttara Nikāya 3.135
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Bhikkhus, what is Right View? Bhikkhus, the knowledge of suffering, the knowledge of the origin of suffering, the knowledge of the cessation of suffering, the…
item.description:
Bhikkhus, what is Right View? Bhikkhus, the knowledge of suffering, the knowledge of the origin of suffering, the knowledge of the cessation of suffering, the knowledge of the path leading to the cessation of suffering. Bhikkhus, this is called Right View.

From: Dīgha Nikāya 22 – Mahāsatipaṭṭhānasutta
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: And, bhikkhus, what is the origination of suffering? Dependent on the eye and forms, eye-consciousness arises. The meeting of the three is contact. With contact…
item.description:
And, bhikkhus, what is the origination of suffering? Dependent on the eye and forms, eye-consciousness arises. The meeting of the three is contact. With contact as condition, feeling arises. With feeling as condition, craving arises. Bhikkhus, this is the origination of suffering. Dependent on the ear and sounds ... on the nose and odors ... on the tongue and tastes ... on the body and tangibles ... Dependent on the mind and mind-objects, mind-consciousness arises. The meeting of the three is contact. With contact as condition, feeling arises. With feeling as condition, craving arises. Bhikkhus, this is the origination of suffering.

From: Saṃyutta Nikāya 12.43
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Bhikkhus, there are forms cognizable by the eye that are desirable, lovely, agreeable, pleasing, connected with sensual desire, enticing. This, bhikkhus, is not called the…
item.description:
Bhikkhus, there are forms cognizable by the eye that are desirable, lovely, agreeable, pleasing, connected with sensual desire, enticing. This, bhikkhus, is not called the ocean in the Noble One's Discipline. Here, this world with its devas, Māras, and Brahmās, with its generations of samaṇas and brahmins, devas and humans, for the most part sink into them, become tangled like a ball of thread, covered like a wound, become like a grass rope, unable to cross the plane of misery, the bad destination, the lower realm, saṃsāra! Bhikkhus, there are sounds ... odors ... tastes ... tangibles ... Bhikkhus, there are mind-objects cognizable by the mind that are desirable, lovely, agreeable, pleasing, connected with sensual desire, enticing. This, bhikkhus, is not called the ocean in the Noble One's Discipline. Here, this world ... for the most part sink into them, become tangled like a ball of thread, covered like a wound, become like a grass rope, unable to cross the plane of misery, the bad destination, the lower realm, saṃsāra!

From: Saṃyutta Nikāya 35.229
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Rāhula, just as a royal elephant with tusks like ploughshafts, massive, well-trained, often found on the battlefield, when in battle uses its forefeet, hind feet,…
item.description:
Rāhula, just as a royal elephant with tusks like ploughshafts, massive, well-trained, often found on the battlefield, when in battle uses its forefeet, hind feet, forebody, hindbody, head, ears, tusks, tail, but protects its trunk. There, the mahout thinks: 'This royal elephant ... protects its trunk; he does not give up his life.' But, Rāhula, when that royal elephant, in battle, uses its forefeet, hind feet, forebody, hindbody, head, ears, tusks, tail, and trunk, then the mahout thinks: 'This royal elephant ... uses his trunk; he has given up his life, and now there is nothing he won't do.' So too, Rāhula, for one who knowingly speaks falsely, without shame and fear, I say there is no evil he will not do. Therefore, Rāhula, 'I will not speak falsely, even for a joke' — thus, Rāhula, you should train.

From: Majjhima Nikāya 61 – Ambalaṭṭhikarāhulovādasutta
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: And, Nigrodha, there are unwholesome states that, if not abandoned, cause defilement, lead to renewed existence, bring suffering, yield painful results, and lead to future…
item.description:
And, Nigrodha, there are unwholesome states that, if not abandoned, cause defilement, lead to renewed existence, bring suffering, yield painful results, and lead to future aging, sickness, and death. I teach the Dhamma for the abandonment of these states. If you practice in accordance with the teaching, then your defiled states will be abandoned, pure states will increase, and you will personally know, realize, attain, and abide in the here and now with perfect and complete wisdom.

From: Dīgha Nikāya 25 – Udumbarika­sīhanādasutta
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: ... Standing to one side, that devata spoke this verse in the presence of the Blessed One: For one fond of conceit, Here there is…
item.description:
... Standing to one side, that devata spoke this verse in the presence of the Blessed One:
For one fond of conceit,
Here there is no taming,
No radiant wisdom,
No concentrated mind,
Living alone in the forest,
With a heedless mind,
He cannot cross beyond
Māra's power.
( Blessed One ):
Abandoning all conceit,
Mind well concentrated,
With mind well examined,
Liberated from all stains,
Living alone in the forest,
With mind not heedless,
That one crosses beyond
Māra's power.

From: Saṃyutta Nikāya 1.9
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: In whatever families, bhikkhus, the parents are honored and revered by the children in the home, those families are considered like Brahmā. In whatever families,…
item.description:
In whatever families, bhikkhus, the parents are honored and revered by the children in the home, those families are considered like Brahmā. In whatever families, bhikkhus, the parents are honored and revered by the children in the home, those families are considered like the ancient teachers. In whatever families, bhikkhus, the parents are honored and revered by the children in the home, those families, bhikkhus, are considered like the devas of old. In whatever families, bhikkhus, the parents are honored and revered by the children in the home, those families are considered worthy of offerings.

From: Aṅguttara Nikāya 4.62
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Good friends, furthermore, here someone has coarse bodily conduct not yet calmed, coarse verbal conduct not yet calmed, coarse mental conduct not yet calmed. After…
item.description:
Good friends, furthermore, here someone has coarse bodily conduct not yet calmed, coarse verbal conduct not yet calmed, coarse mental conduct not yet calmed. After some time, he hears the noble Dhamma, gives proper attention, and attains the true Dhamma and the Dhamma in conformity with it. Having heard the noble Dhamma, given proper attention, and attained the true Dhamma and the Dhamma in conformity with it, his coarse bodily conduct becomes calmed, his coarse verbal conduct becomes calmed, his coarse mental conduct becomes calmed. His bodily conduct being calmed, his verbal conduct being calmed, his mental conduct being calmed, ease arises, and more than ease, joy arises. Good friends, just as from comfort joy is born, so too, good friends, because his coarse bodily conduct is calmed, his coarse verbal conduct is calmed, his coarse mental conduct is calmed, ease arises, and more than ease, joy arises. Good friends, this is the second short path to well-being, realized by the Blessed One, the Knower, the Seer, the Worthy One, the Perfectly Enlightened One.

From: Dīgha Nikāya 18 – Janavasabhasutta
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Bhikkhus, these four cases are to be understood with four cases. What four? Bhikkhus, by living together, virtue is to be understood — thus, over…
item.description:
Bhikkhus, these four cases are to be understood with four cases. What four? Bhikkhus, by living together, virtue is to be understood — thus, over a long time, not otherwise, with attention, not without attention, with wisdom, not with poor wisdom. Bhikkhus, by doing a task together, purity of conduct is to be understood — thus, over a long time, not otherwise, with attention, not without attention, with wisdom, not with poor wisdom. Bhikkhus, in times of misfortune, fortitude is to be understood — thus, over a long time, not otherwise, with attention, not without attention, with wisdom, not with poor wisdom. Bhikkhus, by discussion, wisdom is to be understood — thus, over a long time, not otherwise, with attention, not without attention, with wisdom, not with poor wisdom.

From: Aṅguttara Nikāya 4.192
📖 Read this sutta
